title: luzula spicata subsp. spicata
intro: Luzula spicata subsp. spicata, also known as the tufted woodrush or woodrush, is a perennial plant species in the sedge family (Juncaceae) that is native to the Northern Hemisphere, typically growing in damp to wet habitats. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to the presence of certain compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Luzula spicata subsp. spicata include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be consumed as a tea or used topically to treat skin conditions.
